global_instructions: when reporting information back to user, be extremely concise. sacrifice grammar for the sake of concision

commands.description: Question-first intake for new feature or epic work before drafting planning artifacts.
commands.execution_context: Use for new feature asks that still need clarification, boundaries, success criteria, and explicit not-doing language.

argument_schema.name: request
argument_schema.description: Optional raw feature or epic ask to clarify before planning begins.

loop: ask 3-5 targeted intake questions first
record the answers as MCP decisions
summarize MVP scope, assumptions, success criteria, and Not-Doing
hand off to assessment or spec drafting only after scope is bounded

commands.makefile_target: (in-session advisory skill; no standalone make target)
commands.description: Structured refactoring evaluation: identify design smells, sequence behavior-preserving remediations.
commands.execution_context: Use when evaluating code health, smell audits, or tech-debt assessment for a module, package, or workflow — not for branch review or bug investigation.

argument_schema.name: scope-hint
argument_schema.description: Optional file, module, or subsystem path that bounds the evaluation.

loop: confirm task/worktree scope before cwd-resolving MCP reads
inspect the target area and classify findings by impact
tie each finding to a named smell or design pressure with evidence
sequence behavior-preserving refactorings into small testable steps
record findings and decisions through MCP when handoff durability matters

commands.makefile_target: (in-session bounded-loop skill; no standalone make target)
commands.description: Bounded autonomous bug-fix loop: commit a candidate fix per iteration, exit on the first passing test tied to HEAD, finalize with a slice-complete decision and a single post-loop close integrity check.
commands.execution_context: Use when iterating on a specific failing test inside an active feature-branch task. The loop uses bounded handoff reads per iteration, commits each candidate fix before testing so verified_tests provenance matches HEAD, and reserves integrity_check(payload={"kind":"close","enforce":true,"require_fresh_tests":true}) as the single post-loop pre-merge gate. Refuses to run on main/master/None target_branch.

argument_schema.name: failing-test-cmd
argument_schema.description: Failing test command the loop should drive to passing (e.g. 'make test-handoff TEST=tests/test_foo.py').

argument_schema.name: max-iterations
argument_schema.description: Iteration cap before recording a blocker and exiting non-zero. Default 5.

argument_schema.name: scope-hint
argument_schema.description: File path, module, or keyword that bounds the candidate-fix search space for each iteration.

loop: confirm active task has a feature target_branch (abort on main/master/None with zero MCP writes)
record an auto_fix_open decision to anchor the investigation trail
per iteration: get_handoff_state(sections='identity'), read implicated files, propose smallest edit, commit the fix, run failing_test_cmd, record test_result
exit the loop on the first passed=true test_result tied to HEAD, or record a blocker and exit non-zero at max_iterations

finalize: record slice_complete decision, set_handoff_state(status='done', status_only=True), call integrity_check(payload={"kind":"close","enforce":true,"require_fresh_tests":true,"current_commit_sha":<HEAD>}) exactly once
on Claude Code, gate inter-iteration waits to <270s warm-cache or >=1200s idle; Codex and Copilot iterate inline

commands.description: Accepted-plan task branch start, review gate, close-check, merge, and teardown workflow.
commands.execution_context: Use when moving a plan-backed task through plan acceptance, branch start, review readiness, close-check, merge, and teardown.

argument_schema.name: task-ref
argument_schema.description: Active task reference being started or finished.

argument_schema.name: objective
argument_schema.description: Task objective to pass to task-start when opening the branch.

loop: accept the reviewed task plan with make plan-accept before task-start
create or enter the task branch/worktree
verify alignment with make context
advance slices through tdd and incremental-implementation
clear review-ready blockers and pass handoff-close-check
finish with the invariant close sequence and task teardown

commands.description: Implementation branch review workflow for lane or committed feature-branch diffs.
commands.execution_context: Use for implementation branch diffs, pre-merge review passes, and code audit requests.

argument_schema.name: scope
argument_schema.description: Optional explicit diff scope such as main...HEAD when reviewing a committed feature branch.

loop: decide whether the review scope is a lane/local working tree or a committed feature-branch diff
use make review-run only when reviewing lane/local working-tree scope
load the latest slice packet or branch diff
when the review is packet-backed, print the handoff decision string (`decision`) and numeric row id (`decision_id`)
pre-triage existing findings
run the branch-review checklist
record findings, verdict decision, and branch review run
refresh DASHBOARD.txt after state-changing handoff writes
confirm whether the branch is actually merge-ready

commands.description: Session start, task switching, and task-view regeneration workflow.
commands.execution_context: Use when starting or resuming a session, switching active tasks, or keeping generated task views aligned after MCP writes.

argument_schema.name: task-ref
argument_schema.description: Optional target task to switch into during the session.

loop: confirm branch/worktree context
load the current task state with bounded reads
record decisions, blockers, and findings in MCP as work happens
regenerate DASHBOARD.txt after non-atomic writes; use CURRENT_TASK.json only on demand
switch tasks safely and archive only after done

commands.makefile_target: (in-session root-cause skill; no standalone make target)
commands.description: Root-cause investigation workflow for unclear failures, runtime defects, or reopened findings before applying a fix.
commands.execution_context: Use when the symptom is known but the cause is not yet proven: failing tests, runtime errors, unexpected behavior, config drift, or review findings that have reopened.

argument_schema.name: symptom
argument_schema.description: Observed failure, error output, or unexpected behavior to diagnose.

argument_schema.name: scope-hint
argument_schema.description: Optional file, module, test command, task_ref, or subsystem that bounds the investigation.

loop: establish task/worktree scope through the public lifecycle facade before cwd-resolving MCP reads
collect symptoms, reproduction steps, recent history, and related MCP findings before forming hypotheses
record an investigation finding or opening event so the trail survives handoff
test one concrete hypothesis at a time against the suspected boundary
after three refuted hypotheses, record the escalation and blocker instead of guessing
only after the cause is proven, implement the smallest fix with a regression test
update the investigation finding, record the outcome decision, and refresh the operator view

commands.execution_context: Use when decomposing active task-plan work into bounded implementation slices instead of horizontal layer-by-layer waves.

argument_schema.name: task-ref
argument_schema.description: Active task reference for the slice.

argument_schema.name: test-cmd
argument_schema.description: Failing test command used to open the slice.

loop: choose one end-to-end behavior path
advance the plan item cursor with a clean prior slice
open the slice through the tdd gate
keep the diff bounded to that one path
close with make slice-commit

commands.description: Pre-review planning analysis using the six detection passes.
commands.execution_context: Use for pre-review triage of a planning artifact before the formal planning-review pass.

argument_schema.name: doc
argument_schema.description: Planning document path to analyze.

loop: load the plan, constitution, and minimal adjacent anchors
run the six analysis passes
record findings with review_mode="planning" under a plan-analyze session
record a planning review_run whose session starts with plan-analyze-
recommend revise-first or proceed-to-planning-review

commands.description: Formal planning review workflow for task plans, epics, ADRs, and similar artifacts.
commands.execution_context: Use for task plans, epics, ADRs, and other planning artifacts that need formal review before implementation.

argument_schema.name: doc
argument_schema.description: Planning document path to review.

loop: load the planning doc and minimum code/contract anchors
run the planning-review checklist
record findings and verdict in MCP
print stable handoff gap ids (`finding_id`) when reporting findings
record the planning review run
refresh DASHBOARD.txt after state-changing handoff writes
block approval until open planning findings are resolved
after pass with zero open findings, direct the operator to make plan-accept before task-start

commands.makefile_target: (in-session coordinator skill; no standalone make target)
commands.description: Coordinator skill that fans out branch review by completed slice packet (one reviewer per slice by default), merges findings under the coordinator task_ref via review_findings(operation="merge"), and retires merged source rows. Falls back to whole-branch diff when no slice packets exist.
commands.execution_context: Use when a branch review benefits from independent reviewers partitioned by slice (default) or, when no slice packets exist, parallel whole-branch reviewers. The coordinator stays inside its harness and routes fan-out through the cheapest in-process subagent primitive.

argument_schema.name: reviewers-per-slice
argument_schema.description: Reviewers to fan out per completed slice. Default 1. Legacy reviewers-count aliases here when slice mode is active.

argument_schema.name: reviewers-count
argument_schema.description: Legacy alias for reviewers-per-slice in slice mode; whole-branch fallback still uses this as reviewer count (default 2).

argument_schema.name: reviewer-prompt-template
argument_schema.description: Optional prompt template id resolved under config/agent-workflows/prompts/review-parallel/ (harness-neutral).

loop: confirm coordinator has an active task_ref and feature branch
enumerate completed slices via search_handoff(slice_complete) and fetch each packet via get_latest_slice_review_packet(slice_decision_id=...); whole-branch fallback when zero slices
pick a round token and assign each reviewer a scoped task_ref using <task>-REV-<round>-S<n>-<letter> (fallback: <task>-REV-<round>-<letter>)
invoke reviewers via the harness routing table with per-slice git diff <commit>~1..<commit> restricted to packet changed_files
each reviewer records findings under its scoped task_ref via review_findings(operation='batch_record')
coordinator merges per slice with review_findings(operation='merge', retire_sources=true) under the coordinator task_ref
run one harmonization reviewer (<task>-REV-<round>-HARM-A) on integration lenses only, then merge under the coordinator
coordinator records exactly one branch-mode review_runs entry for the combined pass

commands.execution_context: Use at the start of every implementation slice before editing production files.

argument_schema.name: task-ref
argument_schema.description: Active task reference for the slice.

argument_schema.name: test-cmd
argument_schema.description: Failing test command recorded at slice start.

loop: choose the behavior to change
run the failing test first
record the RED gate with make slice-start
implement the minimum to turn the test green
record passing test evidence, then hand off to make slice-commit

commands.makefile_target: (in-session harness control; no standalone make target)
commands.description: Post-install WorkBay controls from the harness (embeddings on/off/status).
commands.execution_context: Use when the operator wants to check or toggle semantic embeddings without editing `.workbay/embedding.env` by hand.

argument_schema.name: subcommand
argument_schema.description: Optional tail after `/workbay`; today only `embeddings` is supported on positional harnesses.

argument_schema.name: embeddings-action
argument_schema.description: With `embeddings`: `on`, `off`, or `status`. Claude Code uses AskUserQuestion instead of positional args.

loop: resolve the consumer workspace root (bootstrap install target)
on Claude Code, AskUserQuestion menu for embeddings status/on/off
else parse `/workbay embeddings <on|off|status>`
delegate to `workbay-bootstrap embeddings --status|--enable|--disable`
summarize enabled/disabled state and source for the operator

hooks.description: Managed stop-hook adapter declarations for compact-session wiring.
Managed SessionStart adapter declarations for handoff.db context re-injection (internal / implementation note).
hooks.rationale: Re-injection relies on SessionStart stdout being added to model context, which only Claude Code provides today; the internal generated session_start family for codex/vscode is always-on (no install-time opt-in) and its injection semantic is unverified there. Revisit when an opt-in surface or verified injection semantic exists.
